top_signals.title: Strait of Hormuz May Open Within a Month if Framework Deal Holds
top_signals.summary: Tehran would restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz to pre-war levels within a month in a framework deal with the U.S. to also include withdrawing U.S. forces from Iran's vicinity, Iranian state television reported on Wednesday.The report said the U.S.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Trump Domestic Shipping Waiver Fails to Drastically Reduce Gas Prices
top_signals.summary: President Donald Trump’s waivers allowing foreign-flagged ships to move oil and fuel between U.S. ports have had little impact on high domestic gasoline prices due to elevated shipping rates and the relatively small fuel volumes transported so far…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Iran and the Forever War Trap
top_signals.summary: In trying to avoid a quagmire in Iran, America found a dead end.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Commission proposes new authorisation for mobile satellite services for EU's resilience and competitiveness
top_signals.summary: European Commission Press release Brussels, 27 May 2026 The European Commission adopted a proposal for the selection of mobile satellite services (MSS) providers who will be authorised to use the harmonised 2 GHz frequency band beyond 2027, when the current licenses expire.

top_signals.title: The Right Way for Europe to Spend More on Defense
top_signals.summary: America should cofinance the continent’s rearmament.
